figures.caption: Fig. 1. The SparseModesNet architecture with a linear skip connection (Us(ω⊙ẑ)) and a neural network branch (WhNN(ω⊙ ẑ)) forming a residual network. The top right illustrates how the mode selector zeros out unselected modes via element-wise multiplication (colored cells are nonzero).
Fig. 2. Relative reconstruction errors for the linear transport with different decoder structures.

Fig. 3. Mode-fitting by different decoders for the linear transport, spanning modes within the leading-r, within seen s candidates, and extrapolation beyond s to assess decoder interpretability. “SMN” indicates SparseModesNet. Once the method fails, it is excluded in higher modes.
Fig. 4. Relative reconstruction errors for KSE using different decoder structures.

Fig. 5. Mode fitting by different decoders for KSE, spanning modes within leading-r, within seen s candidates, and extrapolation beyond s to assess decoder interpretability. “SMN” indicates SparseModesNet. Once the method fails, it is excluded in higher modes.
Fig. 6. (Left) Isosurface and (right) spanwise sliced flow field at a time instant for (a) streamwise and (b) wall-normal velocity components.
Fig. 7. (Left) Spectral decay of POD modes for turbulent channel flow for both velocity components and (right) zoomed view of leading 100 modes.
Fig. 8. Relative reconstruction errors for (left) streamwise velocity and (right) wall-normal velocity of the turbulent channel flow using different decoders.

Fig. 9. Reconstructed POD modes by different decoders for streamwise velocity, spanning modes within leading-r, within seen s candidates, and extrapolation beyond s to assess decoder interpretability.

Fig. 10. Reconstructed POD modes by different decoders for wall-normal velocity, spanning modes within leading-r, within seen s candidates, and extrapolation beyond s to assess decoder interpretability.

Table 3 Selected modes for each decoder in turbulent channel flow for (a) streamwise and (b) wallnormal velocities, ordered by greedy selection order (GreedyQM/CM) or decreasing ω magnitude (SparseModesNet).
